attributes.description: Alerts when freeable memory on an RDS instance falls below an absolute byte floor. Persistent low memory leads to swapping and latency; correlate with DatabaseConnections and SwapUsage.
attributes.name: [AWS RDS OTel] Freeable memory low

investigation_guide.blob:
## Freeable Memory Low

### Triage
1. Check `SwapUsage` — rising swap confirms memory pressure.
2. Review `DatabaseConnections` for connection storms or leaks.
3. Inspect query workload — heavy sorts, large joins, or missing indexes increase memory use.
4. Compare instance class memory to workload; undersized classes are a common root cause.

### Mitigation
- Reduce connection count or enable connection pooling.
- Tune or terminate expensive queries.
- Scale up to a larger instance class with more RAM.

### Note
Set the byte floor based on instance class RAM (default: 256 MB).

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/RDS"
    AND MetricName == "FreeableMemory"
    AND stat == "Average"
| STATS min_freeable_memory = MIN(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeableMemory`)
    BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
// Absolute byte floor — adjust per instance class (default: 256 MB)
| WHERE min_freeable_memory < 268435456
| SORT min_freeable_memory ASC
